sources_audited.note: The cited Census URL resolves, but its rendered page does not expose the claimed six-digit 333618 definition because the query is for 333. The classification is used only as background to the explicitly estimated eligibility primitive, not as quantitative support for e.

The May 2023 BLS 333600 page confirms 92,420 total jobs and the stated production, engineering, management, business/financial, and computer/mathematical employment shares. The packet openly bridges this four-digit employment mix to a six-digit task-exposure proxy.

NIST supports the claimed machine-monitoring, diagnostics, prognostics, quality, yield, and approximately 500,000-machine-tool context, but describes an ongoing research and deployment program rather than observed implementation or labor substitution in LMM engine manufacturers.

The BLS table confirms the 333600 line's output movement from 38.0 to 43.1 and 1.3% annual growth, as well as employment from 92.8 thousand to 88.4 thousand. The packet discloses the four-digit-to-six-digit demand bridge.

EPA confirms diverse nonroad engine applications and separate standards for heavy equipment, marine engines, recreational vehicles, and small equipment. It does not quantify AI exposure, firm eligibility, implementation, operator requirement, or forward demand, limitations the packet substantially discloses.

EIA confirms internal-combustion engines in construction-site mobile power and emergency or backup generation and lists several generator fuels, but supplies no five-year 333618 demand forecast. The packet uses it only as application context within a disclosed proxy.

caveats: The S1 URL is reachable but does not render the claimed 333618 definition; it should be corrected even though it is only classification background for an ESTIMATE primitive.

The a base is a judgmental transformation from four-digit employment shares rather than wage-weighted task evidence for 333618; the PROXY label and bridge are honest, but the numerical range remains weakly anchored.

NIST AIMS demonstrates technical direction and prototypes, not representative five-year implementation rates or verified labor hours avoided at LMM engine manufacturers, so rho remains highly assumption-dependent.

The d5 base combines broad 333600 output projections with application and regulatory context that does not measure 333618 volumes or electrification displacement; its bidirectional bridge and wide range are necessary caveats.
Firm eligibility, qualifying transfers, commercial retention, and surviving operator requirement are bounded estimates without representative transaction, contract, or firm-level evidence.

Treating repeat engine and parts supply as eligible under the default outsourced-activity lens is plausible but requires bottom-up confirmation because diversified OEM plants, captive production, and product-only relationships may materially reduce the eligible firm pool.
summary: Mechanics pass and the record is useful and internally coherent, but it is model-conditioned and depends heavily on disclosed four-digit proxies and bounded judgment. The cited sources support the occupational mix, manufacturing-AI feasibility context, broad output projection, regulatory application breadth, and backup-power use with the limitations noted above. No unsupported or misclassified claim, when corrected or honestly downgraded, is shown to change the LOW_PRIORITY base tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action, so there is no material finding under the frozen test.
